Once the substance reached the ground, it started to spread and propagate rapidly like some kind of moss.
As it grew, it devoured buildings and trees alike, until it covered entire islands.
Forgive me, but I have to interrupt your conversation.
As thought things weren't dire enough, I have further bad news.
My reconnaissance planes have detected the presence of Gazers beyond the general area of the World Expo.
New ones are appearing in nearly every area I've looked, all of them emitting that mysterious substance.
It's likely by now that the Mediterranean and every coast surrounding it is within range of their invasion.
Certain parts of the sea... No, even facilities on the land have been covered in the mist.
While it's hard to tell from afar with my aircraft, I suspect the reason our communications are still offline is probably because the mist has blanketed our radio stations.
Speaking of the mist, it seems those "clouds" seem to be eating up our carrier-based aircraft.
Once an aircraft flies into one, its connection is lost, as though it was literally swallowed.
I'd speculate this is either due to a loss of control of the aircraft, or its outright destruction.
She speaks the truth. I lost several of my own aircraft to those clouds.
They're like beasts, devouring anything that flies into their mouths.
Something hidden within the clouds that is swallowing aircraft, rather than destroying them...
As unscientific as that claim is, it's hard to dispute the reality.
Even the clouds have become our enemies.
Huh? What do you mean by that?
Are you saying we're literally going to have to fight against clouds?
That strange substance is going to cover our sea, our land, our sky, our everything...
This... This can't be happening...
Guichen, do you reckon the same will happen to Vichya and Iris Libre?
From the sound of it, it seems inevitable.
If the Sirens are behind this, it's almost as if... they're trying to create a Mirror Sea in the real world...
We've seen some of these phenomena before inside a Mirror Sea, but never something on this scale before...
It shouldn't be possible to bring something like this into existence...
...Not without some Sacrament of God.
A vision from this one's nightmares has taken form in the real world...
Have I... seen a being of this nature before...?
——It is not enough.
It can't be...
It is... a being that must not be let free upon the world...
Bad news upon bad news upon bad news... A crushing atmosphere enveloped the conference hall.
Then, just as the mood was about to reach its lowest point, a shrill voice sent a chill down everyone's spine.
Ohohohohoho!
What a spectacular view! Ahahahaha!
Did someone jack into our channel again?
Ugh! Why is EVERYONE doing it to us today?!
Wait, isn't that voice...?
It's... Marco Polo!
...You won't believe your eyes when you see this.
Let's head outside. We must confirm this for ourselves.
Everyone rushed out of the Vichya pavilion.
High in the sky, an opulent throne floated in midair, illuminated by the golden radiance of the Skybound Wall.
The seraphic wings extending from its sides cast a deep shadow across the land, an array of complex machinery sitting atop a pedestal within their embrace.
Embedded in the back of the throne was a large gemstone that resembled an eye.
And in the middle of everything was the familiar figure of Marco Polo.
Good day, pitiful mortals.
Is that really you, Marco Polo? What is the meaning of this?
Oh, can't you tell? After all my work making this series of events easy to understand?
The Kingdom of God has been made manifest in the sky and the sea.
He descended upon the land and granted me, His emissary, supreme powers.
Which is to say, from now on you will refer to me as Her Holiness Marco Polo! Ohohoho!
You're not making a lick of sense...
Under normal circumstances, I'd shrug this off as a prank, but not this time. Do you know what you've done to the World Expo?!
You are its architect! You know better than anyone just how much money and resources we poured into the Expo to spread Sardegna's glory!
End this ridiculous farce at once, Marco Polo!
Spread Sardegna's glory? Ah, yes, glory.
How did you expect to achieve that with some small garden party?
And did you say "ridiculous farce"?! How dare you?! This is His great salvation!
It's our chance at peace and unity...
It's the beginning of a blissful, eternal age of glory for all of mankind...
And it was nearly all ruined just because SOMEONE here was a little too perceptive!
As punishment, I will make you all kneel before me! Ohohohoho!
I will give you one last warning.
What you've done is a betrayal of your homeland. It is a declaration of war against the whole world.
Really now? Doesn't matter to me.
How dare you...! Kersaint, take down the Skybound Wall! I will destroy this traitorous fool myself!
"Don't let her get to you, Veneto. The odds are stacked against us right now."
"If Marco Polo truly is responsible for all this, her powers are far greater than any of us here."
"You can't simply throw yourself at her when there's such a clear power gap, Veneto!"
Huh? Commander? ...Forgive me, I let my emotions get the better of me...
Calm down, Veneto.
I know it's hard to admit, but look around you.
If Marco Polo truly is responsible for all this, her powers are far greater than any of us here.
Do you really want to tackle her head-on? Running away is a better option.
Ugh...
Jeanne d'Arc, what's that thing on Marco Polo's head?
That is the Crown of the Holy See. I'm certain of it.
What I want to know is... how did she get her hands on it?
The Crown is supposed to be under Marseillaise's protection!
Oh no... Did Marco Polo steal it?
But then, Marseillaise... and the stronghold on Saint Helena...
You... What did you do, Marco Polo?!
Ohohohohohoho!
What's there to be shocked about? The Crown simply came into the hands of the one who deserves it.
There is no one more deserving of it than me, Marco Polo, the emissary of God.
No, as a matter of fact, you should be thanking me for personally keeping an eye on this little crown.
Of course, you can struggle all you want, but you are no more than pitiful mortals.
Soon, the Kingdom of God will cover the world.
And then, all war and strife will vanish, and humankind may live in a timeless utopia.
...Naturally, I know the fears and misgivings you mortals have.
But it matters not! The great undertaking has been accomplished!
You do not need to know, and I have no obligation to answer you.
All that's left is to wait for the chaotic noise to change into a beautiful hymn.
"The Divine shall descend upon the land."
Ohohohohohoho!
Marco Polo lets out the boisterous laugh of a woman possessed, completely indifferent to those struggling to lift their heads to gaze upon her.
But soon, she seems to lose interest, and flies off into the distance.
Beneath the golden sky, all she leaves beneath her is an ever-expanding expanse of white.

Betrayal?.say: A loud rumbling came from every direction.
With Marco Polo's departure, the Gazers that had previously been floating around aimlessly now started to converge upon the Vichya pavilion.
Marco Polo... Why?
Veneto, now isn't the time to wallow in despair.
I think those Gazers are coming back to finish the job...
I can't believe it... Marco Polo is actually trying to get rid of us for good...
Umm... A large fleet of mass-produced ships has appeared in nearby waters...
Mass-produced ships? Has someone called for reinforcements?
I don't think so... They don't look like Sardegnian ships, and they don't seem to be engaging the Gazers either.
I'll send visuals right away!
Essex forwarded the picture to everyone present.
Huh... Those certainly aren't Siren ships.
I've never seen mass-produced models like these...
Jeanne d'Arc, Foch, what do you two think?
Ugh... The more I look at them, the more I feel like I should know what they are...
Oh! Aren't those the Tribunal's Mechanicus Confessors?! The one they made the prototype of?
And that means... Wow, they mass-produced those?
Joffre, do you know about this fleet?
Well... it does seem to be affiliated with the Tribunal.
Beyond that, this certainly is news to me.
I've never seen them, either, and Lady Clemenceau has told me nothing about them.
Kersaint is close to her, so it wouldn't be surprising if she knew something, but...
Joffre emphasizes the words "close to her," learning in to give Kersaint a pat on the back.
She's clearly trying to get her to talk.
U-umm... These mass-produced ships...
BEEP—
Kersaint, may I have a moment?
Lady Clemenceau... Sorry, things are a little tense right now...
Well, you seem calm enough to talk. Heehee.
Kersaint, take all Vichya Dominion ships and lead them out of the World Expo venue.
I'll see to it that those Gazers are amply distracted, so they won't trouble you.
Understood, Kersaint?
Yes, ma'am!
Thank you for your efforts. I'll be waiting.
That voice... Was that Clemenceau, the Tribunal's representative?
Lead us out of the venue? Why...?
Kersaint, did she tell you anything?
N-no, only that I should lead everyone out...
But those are Lady Clemenceau's orders, so we have to obey...
(If Marco Polo released those Gazers, then... why is Vichya—no, the Tribunal itself...)
(Is that it? Are Clemenceau and Marco Polo in league with each other?)
Lady Clemenceau, what is your goal...?
I think we can all agree that Marco Polo and Clemenceau's actions are related.
Which means those mass-produced ships aren't our friends...
Vichya and Marco Polo are in cahoots... Or so we're being led to believe.
But why would they reveal that at a time like this? If Vichya wanted to betray us, then...
Heh. Talk about unexpected developments, huh?
Sorry we can't fight alongside you all at a time like this...
I'm sure this is the Iris's will, too... If Lady Clemenceau says so, then we have no choice but to obey.
The Vichya Dominion doesn't mean to make an enemy of you all. Please, at least understand that...!
Let's leave aside what the Tribunal REALLY wants for a sec~
If we let you take all the Vichya girls away, then that means the Tribunal's fleet can move freely. Isn't that right?
Commander, might I suggest we detain them here a little longer?
I know I'd feel a lot safer knowing that we have hostages protecting us from an all-out attack.
I rack my brain over Clemenceau and the Tribunal's motives.
The Gazer threat is real and imminent. If we don't act fast, it'll be too late. There's no reason to force ourselves to fight a losing battle, either.
Say there's a traitor. Is it the entirety of the Vichya Dominion? The Tribunal? Or Clemenceau alone?
I need to shake things up a bit, so that we can get the ball back in our court.
Clemenceau's demands? She only asked for us to evacuate to safety...
"If she really means to eliminate us, then she would disable the Skybound Wall."
Sorry, she didn't say anything about the Skybound Wall...
I assume that means it would be fine to leave it up?
Kersaint doesn't seem to be lying. In that case...
Thank you! Umm... May the benevolent Iris bless you in the coming struggle!
Wow. You're just gonna let them leave?
"You could call this a gamble."
"If Clemenceau had some means of disabling the Skybound Wall, I would think she'd have already used it by now."
"And if the Vichya shipgirls are scheming with Clemenceau, then there would be plenty of ways for them to leave this facility safely."
Uh-huh? So that's what you think... But what if your gamble fails?
"I can give as many reasons as I want, but ultimately, the decisive one is faith in our friends."
"Vichya has never done anything hostile toward us."
"It's entirely possible that, like Marco Polo, something is influencing Clemenceau into madness."
"I'm not about to start being suspicious of innocent people just because of that."
"There's nothing more important to me than having faith in you shipgirls."
Faith, huh? Even if we decide to be hostile toward you?
"If there's a way to bring you back to us and keep you from harming people, then yes. Absolutely."
Heheheh, alright, fine. I'll go with your decision.
I'll have faith in you—as the Commander of Azur Lane, and as someone who cares so much about us.
This one concurs, too...
Very well. Whatever the situation may be, it's better to try and change the situation than it is to sit around doing nothing.
I'd still like to have faith in our Vichya friends, too.
Thank you all! Then we'd best be going...
Excuse me, Kersaint. I wish to ask you one question before you leave.
Do you believe that the Vichya Dominion is treading the path of righteousness?
I-I wonder... I wouldn't really know, but...
I envy those who get to choose their path to begin with.
With that, the Vichya shipgirls left the pavilion amidst the rumbling of the approaching Gazers.

sequence: Royal Navy Rally Point Iberian Peninsula - Strait Fortress

Beholder of the Clouds.say: The blaring of anti-raid sirens through the air–
Unidentified vessels spotted! You have entered our domain... Er, the Royal Navy alert perimeter! Identify yourselves!
Repeating! Unidentified vessels, you've...
Miss Warspite, they're not responding.
They're just approaching silently, not even attacking... Should I keep yelling at them?
Please do. Rather than being the first to attack, we should patiently work to understand the situation.
Roger!
Miss Warspite, we've received word that giant, anomalous machines have appeared in nearby waters and along the coast!
It seems they can move through the sea of their own volition... What should we do about them?
I can see them even from here.
Do we know anything about them?
They don't look like Siren weapons, and we think they aren't made from any other faction's technology... In short, no...
Understood. Are we able to communicate with other bases?
We're doing our best to restore the connections now.
We made contact with the Malta base for a moment and learned that they were in a similar situation, but we quickly lost the connection again.
Unfortunately, we still haven't restored contact with the World Expo venue or Royal mainland.
According to wide-area recon, the seas between the Mediterranean and the NA Sea are covered in fog.
It extends from surface to sky like a wall, making it impossible to measure its height.
We've sent mass-produced submarines, too, but...
We lost control of recon ships and submarines alike the moment they entered the foggy area. They're lost at sea.
It's possible that we may be trapped in the Mediterranean.
Oh, no... It's like the clouds combined and turned into a big, scary monster.
The same thought occurred to me. This is an extremely abnormal situation, so we should be cautious.
So we should stay calm no matter what, huh?
The unusual substance emitted by those machines continues to spread. At this rate, it's only a matter of time...
York, have you warned that mass-produced fleet that we will attack them if they don't identify themselves?
Yes! I did, but they haven't responded whatsoever!
Then they are the enemy.
We have no more need for warnings. Her Majesty told us to protect this area, and that is exactly what we will do.
All ships, obliterate the enemy fleet! You have the fortress's defenses to support you!

sequence: Royal Navy Rally Point Temporary Malta Base

Chivalry and Sacraments.say: Act fast, everyone!
Use active radars to help locate and mop up that gross, gooey substance!
Cold start all inactive radars! Hurry, hurry!
...Ark Royal, how are things on your end?
No enemies coming our way... But the Sardegna Empire isn't looking good at all.
Nelson, any idea what the properties of that goo might be?
Not a clue. It looks like fungus, but also has metallic properties. It doesn't burn, and it's surprisingly tough.
What are we supposed to do about this...?
Are there any casualties?!
No. Fortunately, it's just... kind of gross.
Rodney, Jervis, Janus, and Fortune are working to clean it up as we speak.
You called~?
We're doing just fine here! In fact, we just discovered our secret weapon!
Secret weapon? What have you done?
It's the souvenir shampoo we were selling at the World Expo!
We didn't sell much, so we got to take some of the stock home for free...
Imagine our surprise when we learned that it dissolves that gooey stuff right away!
That's more than just surprising! *sigh*... I'll come over and help.
So it's weak to shampoo? Odd, but dealing with it is our first priority for the moment.
I'll pass this information on to Her Majesty and the Commander right away.
Defeated by shampoo, of all things...
Excuse me. May I take a sample with me?
We have a whole pile of it outside. Take as much as you... Wha?!
Le Terrible?!
Yes, that is my name.
*sigh*... Where did you come from?
Call it a... secret passage.
More importantly, I come with a message from Cardinal Richelieu.
I wish to speak with whoever is in charge of this fortress. Could you tell me who that is?
If Illustrious is at the venue... then would you happen to be in charge?
Sure, but what's this about the secret passage?
Are you saying the Royal Navy aren't the only ones who know about it?
We Iris happen to have a history with this place, as well.
Since there are sacred objects hidden here, it is our duty to protect it.
Well, whether the Cardinal asks us to or not, we're here to secure an escape route for our allies fleeing from the venue.
That goes for my little sisters, too.
R-right... Well, please allow me to assist you.

Counterattack from the Capital.say: A woman was basking in the tranquility of the ancient temple with her eyes closed.
That is, until the silence was broken by a girl's energetic voice.
Roma!
I finally found you! What are you doing here?
Ah. Leonardo da Vinci?
Are you really not going to attend the World Expo? I heard today is closing day.
That's what I was going to ask! Aren't YOU gonna be at the closing ceremony?
No. They changed things so Marco Polo will be delivering the closing speech instead of me.
I don't want to get swept up in anything big again. Not after that unexplained incident at the start of the Expo.
Really, don't worry about me. Go enjoy the rest of the expo.
Trust me, I really want to go! But, I can't!
I made a promise to Alfredo not to leave you by yourself. While she's back at the Expo, I have to stick with you.
But, if you go to the Expo, then I can come along! Come on, you can be there as a spectator – you don't have to play an active role!
As a spectator, you say... Hmm. Let me think for a bit...
Sure. I'll call Alfredo in the meanwhile♪
...That's odd. Why won't it connect?
Is she on the line with someone? Or is she out of range?
That shouldn't be possible with the shipgirl-only channel, though...
BEEP—
It connected! ...Wait, it's not my communicator ringing, but rather Roma's?
This is Roma. Who am I speaking to?
I see you're in the Pantheon! I've been trying to get a hold of you!
Marco Polo? I thought you had a closing speech to deliver? You don't have time to call–
Things have changed. The World Expo is in serious danger.
No, not just the Expo, but the Sardegnian nation in its entirety.
In accordance with the state of emergency protocol, I am tasked with resolving this situation.
Until the state of emergency is lifted, all military forces in and around Sardegna are under my command.
Roma, you are to head to the Expo immediately, prepare for battle, and wait for further instructions.
Is Veneto aware of the situation?
I can't say. The Expo is a mess right now and I don't know where she is.
...Okay.
So you, the woman meant to deliver the closing speech, have abandoned that duty and are issuing orders to shipgirls.
Even supposing our Senate's state of emergency protocol was revised as you've described, I cannot accept your command based solely on what you've told me.
Hmm... Didn't you always have a disdain for politics? When'd you get so smart about this stuff?
Simple – my gut's telling me that you can't be trusted.
There's been something strange in the air, ever since the Sirens kidnapped me during the opening ceremony.
That feeling intensified when you called me. The sanctity of this place, the time-honored history and customs of Sardegna... It all changed the moment you came into the picture.
Whoever you are, you are not Marco Polo.
You've seen right through me, have you? Magnificent! Then let me tell you something.
This place, all of it, has become the Kingdom of God. Once His descent is complete, all war will end.
Until then, take care and be patient! Ohohohohoho!
After a boisterous laugh from Marco Polo, massive explosions could be heard coming from outside the Pantheon.
Holy crap! Where did that giant robot come from?!
It's... the coolest thing ever... Wait, now's not the time!
It's coming to attack us! Roma, you need to get out of...
The horns of war have sounded.
Da Vinci, prepare for battle! We're going in!

Confusion Across Taranto.say: Whoooa! Giant dandelions are falling from the sky!
Whatever those are, they're obviously NOT dandelions!
Bleehhh... Since when did the Sirens start fielding disgusting weapons like this?
The area around Taranto is a mess... Worse yet, I can't get in touch with Veneto or Littorio.
Abruzzi, don't you think we should set out to battle?
No. Best not to jump the gun before we know what's going on.
Rash decisions have cost us too many times in the past.
Good point... Then hopefully, someone will contact us very soon.
BEEP—
This is Marco Polo. Do you read me?
Oh! It's you, Marco Polo!
Thank goodness. This is Aquila speaking. Are you still at the World Expo?
How's the situation over there? Do you have any idea what's going on?
I had a feeling you knew something bizarre is happening.
As you can already tell, all of Sardegna is in serious danger.
In accordance with the state of emergency protocol, I am tasked with resolving this situation.
Until the state of emergency is lifted, all military forces in and around Sardegna are under my command.
Aquila, you and everyone else at Taranto are to head to the Expo immediately, prepare for battle, and wait for further instructions.
To provide rescue and relief efforts? Understood.
Actually, wait... If I remember right, Veneto takes precedence over you on the chain of command on the state of emergency protocol...
What happened to her? Did she confer authority to you?
Veneto this, Veneto that... It's always about her, isn't it?
That's precisely why I've chosen this path.
You know what? Screw all of you! Hmph!
BEEP—
Hm? H-hello? Marco Polo?
Aren't you going to tell us what's going on?
It seems safe to say something quite bad is going down.
It'd be one thing if this was just another quarrel between them, but this clearly goes way beyond any feud.
That strange substance continues to spread even as we speak.
Bad news! Really bad news, everyone!
Look outside! Some ginormous three-legged robot appeared out of nowhere!
It's releasing a white mist everywhere, and it's coming towards us!
A white mist? It can't be!
That robot must be the source of the spore-like substance gumming up the sky!
Abruzzi! Da Recco! Let us prepare for battle at once!

sequence: Vichya Dominion fleet - Immediately following World Expo evacuation

The Master Plan.say: Having escaped from the World Expo, the Vichya fleet had ended up somewhere in the Mediterranean Sea.
The Gazers did not even try to launch an attack on them, just as Clemenceau had promised.
You know, it's gonna look like we're the bad guys, since we fled the Expo after it was attacked.
Kersaint, there's no one else around now. I think it's time you explained everything.
What the heck is going on? And what is Lady Clemenceau after?
I haven't the faintest idea, myself.
Lady Clemenceau said she had an ingenious plan in store, but that's all.
She wouldn't tell anyone what the plan is.
All she would tell me is that everything will go well if we do as she says.
Only Kersaint would naively trust that plan with so little information given.
A plan she kept secret not only from the Senate, but also her own comrades within the Tribunal.
I can only pray this plan will lead to something good.
BEEP—
I see you've arrived at the first set of coordinates, Kersaint.
Has everything gone well so far? The Commander left you alone, just as I said, no?
Yes! We wouldn't have been able to leave the pavilion, had the Commander not been there.
We're fortunate that the Commander is a truly kind person.
Not just kind, but reasonable and keen as well.
Now, I'll send you the next coordinates. Make sure you're ready for battle before you head there.
That being said, you are under no circumstances to engage anyone or anything you encounter until you reach the coordinates.
I will call you again once you arrive.
Understood, Lady Clemenceau.

A Small Lead.say: Seems we've hit the jackpot.
So this is where the Senate has been secretly storing all those artifacts. Inside an inconspicuous warehouse.
What are your thoughts on this, Torricelli?
I don't... really have any?
As I told you earlier... I'm not exactly an artifact appraiser or anything...
If you want an expert opinion... you should go to da Vinci...
Cut me a break. It's impossible to get a hold of her.
Hmm... True. She's always busy with her inventions...
But... it's quicker to send me underwater than her, so there's that...
At any rate, your intuition is reliable.
Veneto and I have had our suspicions for a long while, but couldn't find any leads no matter how hard we tried.
Which is why I want you to search this place from top to–
On that note, Littorio...
It's dark and deserted in here... Same environment as always, so...
Don't make any sound... while I'm investigating...
Hehe, hehehe... I promise I'll be thorough...
Littorio turned on her heel and was just about to leave the warehouse, when...
...The black sun. First it came for Roma, and now it's come for me.
But why? I haven't touched any dubious artifacts, so I shouldn't be seeing this.
Maybe you don't see it in response to touching an artifact, but rather because someone actively shows it to you?
Then who would that be? The Sirens? Or maybe...
BEEP—
It's been a while since we spoke, Littorio.
That voice... Is that you, Clemenceau?

Lives Held Dear.say: The Vichya Dominion and Iris Libre formed a coalition fleet to respond to the imminent crisis. However...
Return to the anchorage?! You can't be goddamn serious, Clemenceau!
That unknown substance will cause unimaginable damage if we don't do somethin' about the enemy!
We're shipgirls, dammit! Our duty is to protect humanity!
Yes, I'm well aware. That is why I'm telling you to return to the anchorage.
Don't let the lives I saved from the Commander go to waste.
I'm not telling you not to fight, alright? If the Gazers launch a direct attack on Toulon, then you may counterattack.
Piss off! You think I'm just gonna sit with my thumb up my ass while everything goes to hell around me?!
For goodness' sake... I'm tired of repeating myself over and over.
Gascogne, be a dear and "persuade" her, will you?
Jean Bart. Requesting that you accept the anchorage plan.
Continued refusal will result in Gascogne engaging coercion protocols.
Tsk... So what the hell are you trying to achieve here?
Don't worry, I'll explain once everything is over. Be a good girl, now.
...God dammit!
Jean Bart smashed her communicator into her rigging's armor, leaving a dent in it.
Taking your anger out on your communicator won't solve anything. If it breaks, you'll need to get it repaired.
You'd be pissed too if you were in my shoes.
I'm not 'bout to train my guns on an ally... We'll head to that damn anchorage.
Please don't feel too downcast, Jean Bart. I would suspect Clemenceau has a plan.
You are blessed sister ships, after all. She must want what's best for–
No. Clemenceau's changed.
She's not the same person I used to know.
Still, don't you think you should have faith in her?
I should. But right now, I can't.
She saved me once, but I can't forgive her for what she's done.
When I stepped out of the freezing-cold water and opened my eyes again, I made a promise to myself...
That I'd make a different decision.
That I wouldn't let the Reenactments play out once again.
That I wouldn't let my friends become puppets and pawns ever again.

Uphill from Here.say: I stepped out of the Vichya pavilion and looked all around me.
The mysterious substance was still growing, stretching, reaching out like a living mycelium that sought to pull heaven and earth into one.
In addition to the Gazers, smaller enemies began to appear with greater frequency as well.
Fortunately, between the Skybound Wall and the alliance fleet's growing cohesion, the combat situation around the pavilion was slowly stabilizing.
However, just reaching a standstill could hardly be considered a victory with how quickly the situation everywhere else was deteriorating.
...Our analysis of the mysterious substance has brought something new to light.
It may just point us to where these smaller enemies are appearing from.
I check the two pictures taken by Illustrious' recon planes that popped up on my phone.
The first shows a place covered in white, dotted with hundreds of cocoon-like objects of varying sizes.
Each cocoon is connected by a small thread, almost resembling cells making up a larger organ.
The second picture shows a cocoon rupturing, an indescribable enemy emerging from it.
That is disgusting... What ARE these things?
Beings that consume all matter, giving birth to abominations, multiplying...
It is like a scene out of a nightmare, something that could not exist in reality... Even with the Commander by my side, I feel fear...
We may be in a new type of Mirror Sea... Or perhaps, even inside this one's dream...?
Not to be rude, but that just sounds like wishful thinking to me.
"Don't take that personally, Shinano. Others don't understand the power you have, but I do."
No... She is correct... I have no evidence, therefore it is natural that I be doubted...
Reality as this one knows it and the reality we are in have diverged drastically since the arrival of these Gazers...
Supposing that this is a dream, it would explain Marco Polo's and Clemenceau's unusual behavior...
What we do not yet know is if you and this one are the only dreamers, or if all shipgirls are...
That reminds me... Shinano said the same thing back in AF – that the events of that Mirror Sea were a dream.
Of course, no sane person would believe that. There is no way that an anomaly that has tangible effects on both people and reality is only a dream.
However, it's a different story if we assume we've been pulled into a space which functions like a new kind of Mirror Sea, rather than just a dream.
It's a very optimistic guess, but approaching the situation this way is the only way we can hope to resolve it.
"First of all, we're going to treat this space – including the Gazers, the goopy substance, even the World Expo – as part of an anomalous Mirror Sea."
"To free ourselves from it, we will need to find whatever controls the Mirror Sea and destroy or neutralize it."
"That could be Marco Polo, or it could be a machine of some sort. Either way..."
...Commander! Our signal is online!
We have connected directly to the Space Needle tactical line and can communicate with all bases within a huge radius!
Starting up cross-pavilion data transfer line and signal array now!
Royal Navy, Iron Blood, Sakura Empire, Sardegna Empire, Dragon Empery, Northern Parliament, Iris Libre... standby!
Three... two... one... Helena! Turn it on!
BRRRRZZZZZ... fortress here... BRRRRZZZZZ.... received... BRRRRZZZZZ... respond... BRRRRZZZZZ...
Malta... BRRRRZZZZZ... Expo... BRRRRZZZZZ... please respond... BRRRRZZZZZ...
There was a reason I had everyone avoid combat with the Gazers and go through the risk of eliminating the enemies inside the Expo venue.
It was so we could connect to the Space Needle and amplify our signal, establishing a communications network with all bases around the Mediterranean Sea.
The flow of incoming signals all but overloaded the line. This meant our allies were out there, fighting as we spoke.
At the same time, however, it also meant that this anomalous phenomenon was not contained to just the sea around the World Expo.
"First thing's first – we need to sort the situation out and establish a chain of command."
Leave that to me, Commander.
Two heads are better than one, right? I'll help.
Make that four. Pamiat' Merkuria and I will assist, too.
I'm no expert when it comes to this stuff, so let me instead introduce someone who is.
Thiele, come here and give these people a helping hand.
Very well. I can do that.
You, too, should step forth, Noshiro...
Certainly, Shinano.
Working with our allies across the Mediterranean, we finally managed to get a basic grasp on the situation.
Finally, we could pierce through the fog of war and seize the initiative.
Though Gazers had appeared as far as Gibraltar, the fortress there managed to hold.
I asked Warspite to mobilize what forces she could, and she is now on her way with her main fleet to link up with us.
The situation in Malta, however, is far more dire. The garrison is being besieged, its defenders all but surrounded.
In light of this, I put Eugen in charge of a small but battle-hardened detachment of Iron Blood forces to provide support.
Assuming they're able to repel the enemy and secure the island, the plan for now is that they'll group up and return here.
As for the Iris and Vicha forces in Toulon, Clemenceau apparently requested them to defend an anchorage there.
As far as the intel they shared told us, they have no idea what the Vichya's Holy See fleet and the Tribunal are up to.
So, to keep an eye on Clemenceau, they sent Jean Bart and her fleet away to be on standby.
Then, there was the Sardegna Empire, and their groups were quite split.
Marco Polo declared their state of emergency protocol to be in effect and made calls to all Sardegnian bases to inform the shipgirls there that they were under her control.
None of them believed her, however. Later, Veneto convinced them to join our side.
Aquila's fleet over in Taranto had eliminated all enemies in the surrounding waters, and were now regrouping and resupplying.
Littorio and Torricelli were in Venice, investigating a matter relating to their Senate. Then, when the Gazers appeared, they fled the area.
They then wound up alone inside a bunker, but as they assured me, there was no need to worry whatsoever and they'll find an alternate escape route.
That didn't stop me from worrying, of course. Based on Veneto's relieved response to hearing them, however, I just had to believe they'd be okay.
Then there's Roma and a few others. They were in the capital and had successfully repelled an attack on it.
They said they'd mop up the stragglers then group up with us. More importantly, though, they told me a very important piece of information.
That Marco Polo is performing some kind of ritual on her giant, flying throne.
Finally, there was one Sardegnian fleet near the Oasis port that we were completely unable to contact.
That could just have been due to the weather, since a sandstorm appeared there just before the closing ceremony. Just to be sure, we had Taranto send recon planes there.
All in all, our allied fleet includes everyone at the World Expo, a few Sardegnian fleets around the Mediterranean, scattered Iris and Vichya fleets, and a Royal Navy detachment.
The Gazers are powerful, and they're aided by endless cannon fodder. We are outmatched ten to one... but, there is still a chance.
Now, what's our next move?

Excommunication.say: Mount Vesuvius – the most dangerous active volcano in Europa that once wiped out an entire city in a single eruption.
A woman clad in opulent black robes stood at the edge of a distant cliff, her eyes fixed on the crater that rises 1,200 meters above sea level.
However, the dozens of transparent communication windows flickering in the air around her show that she is not here for sightseeing.
The higher you climb, the farther you'll be able to see.
Take in the fresh air, and let it calm your mind. Up here, you have all the time in the world to wait. There is no need for anxiety.
Now, all the preparations are complete.
All that remains... is for the easterly wind to blow.
BEEP—
Clemenceau! Where are you?! Can you hear me?
Why hello there, Marco Polo. I just arrived near the outskirts of Rome.
All the roads were covered with Divine Vestige, you see. It took quite a bit of time to navigate around all of it.
I told you! You should've set out sooner!
Yes, but I didn't want to raise suspicions. Don't forget that I'm the Tribunal's representative.
*sigh*... You need to get your priorities straight.
That's not like you to say. What's wrong? Have things not gone according to plan?
Well, SOME things have. Beyond that, though, it's been an absolute trainwreck.
It's those damn fools at the Expo... They're hell-bent on struggling until the very end.
Couldn't even convince my fellow Sardegnians either. They wouldn't shut up about Veneto this, Veneto that.
Some of them have even dared to challenge me personally!
And from the look of things, it's not gone so well for you either. Why hasn't the Skybound Wall been deactivated yet?
If turning it off was so simple, it wouldn't be a sacrament.
If you want it gone so badly, why not just use your Oculi Dei? Surely it cannot stand before the machines of true divinity.
Of course! If I used all of their power, it wouldn't stand a chance.
However, I can't. I have more important things to focus on.
God's descent has yet to be completed. I don't have the energy to waste on micromanaging the machines.
This would've all gone like a dream, had the Commander not been here. Then I wouldn't have to do this!
Bah... By the way, the Vichya fleet is waiting for your orders.
Tell them to put some pressure on the Expo.
Remember our promise. If my plan falls apart, yours will as well!
But of course. The Vichya Holy See Fleet is never idle.
I'll call the girls in Toulon right away.
Jean Bart, do you hear me?
What do ya want? You changed your mind yet?
You could say that. You have clearance to sortie.
I take it you're ready and raring to go?
Goddamn right. I can't stand to ignore those giant monsters for one more second.
First, I'm gonna mop up at the Expo. Then, I'm gonna wipe out every last one in the waters around it.
Good, you already have a plan. Yes, your enemy is at the Expo.
Be a dear and smite all the shipgirls at the Expo as well with the help of the Oculi Dei, will you?
...What'd you say?
The hell are "Oculi Dei"? And whaddya mean "smite" them?!
They are the mechanical eyes of God Himself. As for "smite," you don't need me to explain that, do you?
Don't worry. I've already evacuated every Vichyan shipgirl from there.
The only ones left now are the sinners.
The Royal Navy, Eagle Union, Northern Parliament, Iris Libre, Iron Blood, Sakura Empire, and Sardegna Empire...
They're all inhuman warmongers who torment the world with their Reenactments. And then there's the one who leads them all.
We've been hurt, mistreated, trampled, and made to suffer for far too long...
Now, the people responsible are all in one place. Isn't this an ideal opportunity to end them?
All conflict will cease to be without their armies to wage war.
Without conflict, there'll be no reason to divide ourselves into factions and groups.
We shall have our revenge, purge the sinful, and prepare to welcome God.
This is the Last Judgment, Jean Bart.
Clemenceau... Have you lost your goddamn mind?
We are SHIPGIRLS, dammit! Our duty is to PROTECT humanity!
Yes! Reenactments are unfair and unjust!
But I'm not about to kill people because of that! Our enemies are those giant eyeballs, not our own allies!
If we don't do something about 'em, all of civilization is gonna be smothered under that nasty goo!
Even if the world isn't all on our side, we need to stand together as shipgirls and fight the real fight!
Those giant machines, Oculi Dei, whatever – THOSE are the real enemy!
As we speak, they're tryin' to destroy everything we're supposed to protect!
I applaud your spirit. Your heart is in the right place.
However, you're wrong. They haven't "destroyed" anything, and they will not lay hands on that which we're supposed to protect.
As I said, this is Judgment Day. The sinful must be punished, and the seas must be wiped clean to make way for the Kingdom of God.
We will not forget nor forgive the pain we and our faith have suffered.
...I get it now. You're not Clemenceau – you're someone wearing her face like a mask.
Someone's got to put a stop to your psycho plan.
I'm not gonna let these robots go to the Expo, and I'm not gonna point my cannons at my friends!
You're saying you don't want justice?
Your twisted idea of it? No. I don't need it.
I'm not gonna follow any damn Reenactments, and I'm not gonna be controlled like a puppet!
If those machine monsters are gonna go after the people I care about...
Then I'll stop at nothing to kill them, even if it costs me my life!
And if you're on their goddamn side, I'll kill you too!
Ahahahahah! Listen to yourself. I'm the Tribunal's representative – I'm your ally.
Clemenceau's more than that. She is my sister, and she is NOT a servant to some fake God!
Whoever you are, you're sure as hell no ally of the proud Templar Knights!
Neither is that woman with the Crown of the Holy See hiding behind you! The only thing she is is a clown!
"CLOWN"?! HOW DARE YOU?!
You have insulted the emissary of God! A crime worthy of divine retribution!
Out of my way, Clemenceau! I am going to teach this little rat some manners!
Of course. By the name of the Crown of the Holy See, I hereby excommunicate Jean Bart, and all those who side with her, from the Vichya Dominion.
After delivering a punishment equivalent to exile, Clemenceau hung up without another word.
Well, well. That's not a very easterly wind at all.
What will you do now, I wonder, Commander?

Doing What's Right.say: Commander, we just got word that Iris Libre and Vichya Dominion forces have engaged the enemy!
Vichya hasn't sided with Marco Polo after all!
"Finally some good news, but given the Tribunal's involvement, we can't assume that they're on our side just yet."
"On one hand, Clemenceau did threaten us with mass-produced ships and Gazers. On the other, we haven't been formally declared as enemies either."
"The Skybound Wall is still functioning, and it has successfully kept the Gazers at bay."
"Maybe it's just Clemenceau. Maybe she's acting so unusually because she's under some spell..."
Got it! I'll be honest, there is the chance that this is a trap...
But as you said, the rest of them didn't act hostile towards us, and they didn't mess with the pavilion's defenses either.
Right now, we are all allies in the battle against enemies of humanity.
It's possible Clemenceau has lost her mind, or that she's pulling a ruse in typical Tribunal fashion...
Regardless, we are all shipgirls. We're people with a common goal.
"I'm confident that they feel the same way."
"...The Royal Navy fleet should be arriving any minute now. As for that 'secret passage'..."

Eruption.say: Silence swept across the peak of Mount Vesuvius.
This was one place where the white substance – the Divine Vestige – had not made its mark yet.
Battles were being fought across the Mediterranean Sea, but the sounds of war could not reach this tall peak.
Oh, Clemenceau! Where are you now?
I'm on the way to the safehouse you told me about earlier.
The Tribunal's mass-produced ships have arrived, so it's time for me to set off to the Expo.
Magnificent! Finally, some good news.
It's ridiculous how much pushback we've faced when God's descent is so close to completion... Thank goodness I have you to rely on.
Before your final battle, I have something nice to tell you.
I chose the safehouse you'll be staying in, especially for you.
It's separated from the Senate's secret facilities by only one block.
Also, you'll find Flandre waiting for you there.
You heard that right – Flandre, the blueprint ship. The ace up our sleeve, better than anyone else at manifesting religious concepts.
Really? I would've never guessed she was so close by.
Hahaha! I reckoned she'd be of use to you, so I had her redeployed.
Besides, she belongs to the Holy See, right? It only makes sense that you'd command her, not me.
Now go. Rendezvous with Flandre, take the Tribunal's fleet with you, and head for the World Expo!
I'll send Trento META to assist you as well.
Make those nonbelievers see the light of God!
I will, Marco Polo.
Don't worry about me. Focus only on completing the ritual to...
Oh. She hung up.
So you're going to show your full hand this early. Reckless as always, Marco Polo.
And I hoped you'd stay in the game for at least a while longer...
A shame. It truly is a shame.
Clemenceau lightly shook her head in disappointment, then extended a hand up to her holograms and tapped through a menu.
Kersaint speaking. I've arrived at the coordinates. What am I supposed to do now?
I'll tell you. Listen closely. Blueprint ship Flandre will come there to meet you.
She did briefly serve Marco Polo, but I assure you, she's on our side. Whatever you do, don't attack her.
Once she has arrived, call everyone to regroup with you.
Then, you will go to the new set of coordinates I just sent. Be ready for a battle.
Your enemy is a META – Trento.
She'll think you're on her side, so try to catch her off guard.
Be quick and incapacitate her with one blow.
You must not sink her, and you must not let her get away. Just incapacitate her.
Once that's done, call me back, and remain on standby until your next orders.
...Did you get all that, or was it too shocking for you? Shall I start over?
N-no need, I heard you loud and clear! I've committed it all to memory!
By the will of the Holy Iris.
Yes. By the will of the Holy Iris.
Clemenceau terminated the call, then closed all her holograms.
She grasped her staff firmly, held it high...
And at that exact moment, Mount Vesuvius erupted.
The old Tribunal's crowning achievement among conceptual weapons – Blanc, the symbol of Conquest.
Smoke blotting out the sun, flames consuming everything; finally, a horseman to signal the end days.
It really sets the scene for the climactic finale. Heehee.
Now, I'd better leave while I'm ahead of the clock.
Although I've told Marco Polo countless lies, there's one thing I haven't lied about.
That roads covered by the Divine Vestige are truly, truly awful to walk on.

Goodbye, My Friend.say: Atop her winged throne, Marco Polo looked out across the world, watching the final minutes tick away until her audience with God.
The throne and crown both shimmered, signaling that her great ceremony was close to completion.
The seas began churning, and from the dark depths, something began to stir, clawing its way into this reality.
Ohohohohoho! This magnificent feeling! God has come!
Glory to Sardegna! Glory to me!
Let this be the start of a new age!
BOOOOOOM!
A loud, distant rumble drew Marco Polo's attention.
Is that Mount Vesuvius? Why would it erupt now?
More importantly, what is THAT?!
What in the name of... White armor, a bow, and a horse?
How is it flying when it's riding a horse?!
No, what am I saying? That doesn't matter!
The real question is – what in God's name is it, and why did it just leap out of a volcano?!
Marco Polo stared like a deer in headlights at the giant knight, unresponsive to the fact that it was moving closer to her.
What?!
It drew its bow! And it's aiming... right at ME?
Crap! I can't dodge it in time!
KABOOOM!
AAAAAHHHHHH!
Though she herself avoided a direct hit, the horseman's laser beam nevertheless grazed her throne's superstructure, causing a devastating explosion.
Critically damaged from the shockwave and the damage of the explosion itself, the throne began to sway violently.
Damnit, damnit, damnit! My wings! I can't maintain flight!
I need to find... somewhere to make an emergency landing!
BEEP—
Clemenceau's calling? Now, of all times?!
Clemenceau! Where are you?!
You must have seen the giant horseman by now! What IS that thing?!
Is it some secret weapon? Are Veneto or the Commander in control of it?!
I need help! My throne is breaking down as we speak!
Forget about the Expo! Come here and rescue me!
"I saw the first seal open, and I beheld a white horse."
"And he that sat on him had a bow, and a crown was given unto him, and he went forth conquering."
That is Blanc, the Tribunal's Mechanicus Harbinger of Conquest, a divine machine made by mortal hands.
Now, with the concept of conquest strengthened, I have regained control over the Tribunal's mass-produced ships.
Checkmate, Marco Polo.
WHAT?! Was this your goal all along?!
You lied to me! You manipulated me, Clemenceau!
Ahahahaha! Took you long enough to realize.
While you're a good friend, your "God" was never meant to be.
How could you do this? How could even YOU betray me, Clemenceau?!
We shared the same dream! We were kindred souls! You were the only person I could trust!
I thought you were with me... I thought you were my friend...
Don't sulk. I feel the same about your dream, and I'm thankful for all you've done for me.
Likewise, our friendship wasn't a lie. I still consider you a friend.
And that is precisely why I have to stop you.
This is the only way to save Sardegna's future, as well as your life.
Why... Why would you do this to me?
You will never be more than a jester, and I mean that in a good way. You're not fit to play a villain.
A villain betrays. They betray their beloved nation, their family, and their friends.
If you want my advice – let the true villain take on all the responsibility.
While it is true that the nation of Sardegna has made mistakes, it is only a victim at the end of the day.
In fact, actively cooperating with the Commander may be to its benefit.
Don't you think that would be a great first step towards spreading your glory?
In the end, you're just a mortal... like the rest of them...
That I am. A mortal and a villain. But, even a villain can look out for her friends.
Listen, Marco Polo. I will not let you summon a false god that will turn you into a Pawn.
I will not let you destroy everything you hold dear.
And most of all, I will not let you be tarnished with sin beyond redemption.
There's no point... Nothing matters anymore...
Why... Why did this have to happen...
I was so close... Just one step away...
It's over... My ideals...
My... perfect world...
Muttering in disillusionment, Marco Polo and her crumbling throne sank beneath the water's surface.
Once more, silence swept across the world.

Raising the Holy Banner.say: A massive fleet was gathering in the waters outside of Saint Helena's port.
Before Richelieu's eyes, countless Iris Libre flags fluttered in the wind.
But among them was a flag that had not been flown for what felt like an eternity – the Iris Orthodoxy's insignia, representing both Iris Libre and the Vichya Dominion.
All who saw it couldn't help but wonder – how long has it been? How long have they waited for this moment?
Richelieu's eyes turned from the flags raised all around her, towards the sea fog hanging low in the distance.
What had once been a source of mystery and doubt was now a doorway towards her goals.
But unlike the sunny, placid seas around her that shimmered with the colors of her homeland, what awaited on the other side of the doorway was the scent of gunpowder and burning debris.
(Le Terrible, this is what you were searching for at the Tribunal stronghold...)
(But why does the Tribunal use Siren technology to this extent?)
(Clemenceau, what role do you play in all of this...?)
Cardinal, it's almost time.
Yes. At long last...
Richelieu took a deep breath, stepped forward in front of the podium, and felt the weight of her fingertip pressing down into the tiny button on the microphone.
My friends of the Iris Orthodoxy, listen well–
The entire audience fell silent as everyone's attention shifted to the Cardinal's words.
Richelieu looked out across the people gathered there. She saw her friends – those who she had fought alongside before, and those who she would fight alongside in the battle to come.
My friends, you have seen what lies on the other side of that door–
Our blue seas, violated by the enemy.
Our land, rivers, and skies engulfed in a nightmare from which there is no waking up.
As we speak, the anomalies that attacked the World Expo encroach on our world.
The flames of war threaten to consume us once again.
But do not forget – there are still those who would also give anything to snuff out those flames.
Yes, our compatriots in the Vichya Dominion are fighting as we speak.
Richelieu gestured to the doorway in front of them. The scenery on the other side became clearer and more vivid as the sea fog prepared to teleport them.
Cannons firing desperately, swarms of bizarre enemies, giant machines raining from the sky, a sea choked with unnatural, grasping clouds.
Do not forget – both Iris Libre and the Vichya Dominion used to exist as one.
The Iris Orthodoxy has always been one.
However, the misfortunes of the past divided us, misleading us into turning our blades on our own.
We never asked for those scars that now cover us and our homeland.
None of us chose the unreasonable reality we now face today.
After a pause, she surveyed her compatriots again.
Then, she reached for a flag standing on the podium—one that she did not normally bear.
Despite that, many of you have followed me across the Royal Islands, through the flames of war.
Others joined us along the way on this long march toward justice.
I, Cardinal Richelieu, firmly believe that we are all driven by the same belief, regardless of how long we have been on this journey.
There is no doubt in my mind that we are all gathered here for a unified purpose–
To see the Iris Orthodoxy become whole once more!
We will not turn a blind eye while our home is ravaged!
We will not turn a blind eye to the suffering of our friends!
We proud warriors will not bend. Not to Sirens, not to the Reenactments, and certainly not to the fear and uncertainty.
Even if what lies beyond this door is a calamity that threatens all of civilization, there is but one thing for us to do.
We will defy it, and we will fight in the name of the Iris Orthodoxy to the very end!
I, Cardinal Richelieu, do solemnly swear upon this sacred banner...
I will fight until I breathe my last.
I will fight until the banner of the Iris Orthodoxy flies free over our homeland, when no enemy shall ever threaten us again!
Come, my compatriots of the Iris Orthodoxy. The time is upon us.
Weigh anchor! We set sail for our motherland!
Their vessels rumbled like thunderous applause.
Thus, the glorious march of a unified Iris Orthodoxy had begun.
Fierce fighting had been raging on for quite some time.
Burning wreckages now littered the surface of the water.
Though the defenders had managed to buy a moment of peace, there was no doubt in their minds that they would not be able to hold off the next onslaught.
After destroying the final enemy in her line of sight, Jean Bart kneeled, exhausted, and looked at her injured allies.
What drove them was no longer pride, but an indignation of not wanting it to end like this.
Not again, damn it...
Am I just losing control of my emotions...
...Or did I make the wrong decision?
Did I lead my friends to destruction again?
Jean Bart, the next wave is coming!
It's not just mass-produced ships this time! They have more forces in the skies above and waters below...
What should we do...?
(Good question... I damn sure wish I knew the answer.)
(First Richelieu, now Clemenceau. Why must we suffer betrayal after betrayal?)
Jean Bart numbly pushed away the call from her communicator and sat down in the water.
Don't give up just yet, Jean Bart.
Sister...?
Tch... What, is that just my life flashing before my eyes? Or is it the stupid black sun making me hallucinate?
What a cruel joke. So this is who God sends to take me away.
Jean Bart, as leaders, there are always those who look towards us for hope.
If even we give up, we shut the door on those who deserve a brighter tomorrow.
Please fight until the last light has faded.
Quoting the Commander, huh? God sure has a funny sense of humor, using my sister's face to say those words...
BOOOOM!
The booming of artillery heralded the return of war to the quiet sea.
The anomalous enemies born from Divine Vestige appeared on the horizon once more.
However, their attacks were not directed toward Jean Bart's fleet.
Jean Bart, reinforcements! Reinforcements have finally come!
Look over there! It's the Iris Libre fleet, along with the Tribunal's fighting angel!
The enemy is being routed! We're saved, Jean Bart!
Phew... I'd just made peace with the fact that I was about to get sunk. Talk about good timing...
But, if both the Tribunal and Iris Libre are here, does that mean... that the conflict with Clemenceau was just an elaborate ruse?
Jean Bart could not make sense of the words she was hearing.
Her emotions had been roiling for so long. What did it all mean? Could she afford to have hope again?
Iris Libre... and the Tribunal, together...? What's going on...
And when I heard Richelieu's voice before... Was that...
Sorry I'm late, Jean Bart.
No matter how desperate the situation is, we must not give up on hope.
Otherwise, we will not be able to seize that brief glimmer of light when it finally arrives.
Cardinal Richelieu... Why are you here?
You didn't come just to laugh at me, did you?
No, I'm here to save someone dear to me.
A certain shipgirl who calls me her sister, in fact.
Though, it wouldn't hurt to call me that more often.
For example... During a touching reunion? Such as this one?
Repeat after me, now. "Sister."
Why, you...!
Sorry, I got a bit carried away there.
I'm sure there's a lot you'd like to get off your chest right now, but the enemy is still out there.
I'd love to catch up with you later, but let's get right to business first–
Le Terrible told me about the situation at the World Expo venue. We're going to support the Commander next.
But, I can only speak for Iris Libre. The Vichya Dominion will need to make its own choice.
Jean Bart, will you join me?
...I don't speak for the Vichya Dominion anymore.
We've been excommunicated by Clemenceau. We're just exiles now.
That doesn't change my offer.
Jean Bart stood up, took a deep breath, and flipped the switch on her communicator.
To all those shipgirls who no longer have a place to call home – I am battleship Jean Bart, formerly of the Vichya Dominion, speaking!
I will now join Cardinal Richelieu's fleet, and aid her in fighting for a unified Iris Orthodoxy.
I'm no longer in any position to order any of you around, but...
I hope you'll decide to join us in driving those bastards out of our homeland!
Jean Bart...!
I'd be glad to!
Of course. Banished or not, we'll follow you to the ends of the earth.
Haha, you stubborn old grouch! I'm just glad you and Algérie finally decided to open up!
...Yeah, it's been too damn long.
It's time... to put the past behind us.
Jean Bart discarded the shattered banner of the Vichya Dominion, and got down on one knee in front of Richelieu.
Richelieu then lay her own Iris flag atop Jean Bart's shoulder.
As Cardinal of the Iris Orthodoxy, I solemnly declare...
Jean Bart, your unjustly-deprived status as a Templar Knight and your position within the Iris Orthodoxy's fleet are hereby restored to you.
The same goes for all the other shipgirls here formerly under the command of the Vichya Dominion.
Please fight alongside us, Jean Bart.
By the will of the Holy Iris...
I swear my allegiance to you, Cardinal Richelieu. Sister.
...I've finally come back to you, my sister.
Sorry it took me so long.
Cheers erupted over the ocean.
And, without anyone's notice, Gascogne silently slipped away after bearing witness to it all.

Where It All Began - Part I.say: *sigh*... Would you just answer me, Friedrich?
What do you think of the Reenactments under these circumstances?
We'll never be able to get rid of the Sirens for good unless we all join forces.
Naturally. But your proposal isn't grounded in reality.
As long as the Naval HQ has influence, any forceful attempt to unite the factions will end in failure.
Even a superficial alliance will come to naught, just like the previous Azur Lane.
Squeezing sand in your hand will only cause it to slip through your fingers...
Until nothing remains.
Hmph. Alright, so we can't get along. But that doesn't mean we can just go along with the Sirens' plans.
Doesn't that only fuel their power and make the situation worse?
War is but a continuation of politics with other means.
What is true of Reenactments... is also true of our war against the Sirens.
It's simply not time yet, Marco Polo. Don't rush things.
I'd best be going soon. Return to Sardegna, and play your part of the composition.
Friedrich left. Only Marco Polo remained in the room.
You sound so self-important, Friedrich der Große.
Is it because you're a blueprint ship? Well, so am I!
Don't we exist to crush Sirens with overwhelming force?
*sigh*... Wasting energy on politics and inter-faction conflicts is so inefficient. Bismarck's confidant, my ass.
But it's true that we're getting nowhere fast right now. There has to be a way to break through this, like a way to easily beat the Sirens...
I know... I'll just have to find someone stronger than them.
Just you watch, Friedrich. Someday, I'm going to clean up this mess myself.
Then, the whole world will know my name and marvel at my greatness!

Curtain of Night.say: With the fall of Marco Polo's throne, everything had changed in an instant.
The sky went dark at once, as if a purple curtain had been drawn over it. Beneath the artificial night sky, fear and uncertainty continued to grow despite the "victory."
Clearly, things had not gone back to normal even with Marco Polo's defeat.
Danger was still growing within the darkness.
BEEP—
Good day– Or rather, I suppose I should say "good evening" now.
Did everyone enjoy Marco Polo's curtain call just now?
Commander, umm, we seem to have a call from Clemenceau.
That's right. It's me, Clemenceau from the Vichya's Tribunal.
I would've loved to meet all of you and enjoy our time together at the closing ceremony...
But alas, who could've anticipated all of this to happen?
How shameless... Acting like you weren't a part of this.
You were also the one who sicced those mass-produced ships and Gazers on us, weren't you?
Right before they landed, you transferred all of your own Vichya ships away!
You're not going to giggle and say all of this was just an act, are you?
She's right! You better give us a believable explanation for everything that happened!
Oh my, is that the name you came up with for my divine machines?
That's cute. Heehee.
Clemenceau, don't change the subject.
As the host of this World Expo, I demand an explanation for this catastrophe.
Heehee. It WAS all an act~ ...You'd be surprised how close you are to the truth.
The situation necessitated the emergency transfer.
Had I not done so, Marco Polo would've attempted to coerce them into betraying you, and I would've not been able to release the Horseman of Conquest.
Likewise with the Tribunal's mass-produced ships. They were all a façade to convince her that I was on her side.
You understand, don't you, Commander? The Skybound Wall could easily withstand the divine machines' attacks.
My "transgressions" did not truly place you in harm's way, so why not put on a show to earn Marco Polo's trust?
In other words... You knew her plan from the start, and you've been pretending to work with her all this time.
All to stop her in the end...?
Exactly. I feigned cooperation until her plan reached its very climax.
Didn't you think the shot that perfectly picturesque?
I must admit, it was quite magnificent... But what was that golden-armored machine?
Is it related to the divine machines you were talking about?
Somewhat. That is a weapon called the Mechanicus Harbinger, developed in secret by the Tribunal.
You may be able to trace its roots back to Marco Polo, but rest assured that it is under my control.
...I suppose we should content ourselves with the knowledge that they won't attack us anymore.
But Clemenceau, do not assume that everyone will be satisfied with that alone.
The Marco Polo I know would never come up with such a dangerous scheme.
If you cooperated with her, having full knowledge of the danger of her schemes, then that makes you a co-conspirator... No, worse. You enabled her.
That's hardly fair, Vittorio Veneto.
Even knowing her plans, do you think I had the capacity to stand up to the power she wielded?
If there was even the slightest leak, her first order of business would be eliminating the Tribunal, given our specialty in information manipulation.
Regardless, we still haven't cleaned up the mess she made.
I'll have my subordinates recover her. For now, shall we meet face-to-face?
...Though I'll have you know that I'm already on my way to the Vichya pavilion.
"We just learned that Jean Bart's fleet has been struggling to fight off the enemy assault for some time now."
"A Royal Navy fleet is on their way to reinforce them, but the enemies on the way have slowed them down. We don't know how well that secret passage will continue to function, either."
"If the Tribunal has mobile forces to put to work, then I expect you to stop the enemy reinforcements heading to Toulon."
Yes, of course, Commander.
Toulon is in Iris territory, and Jean Bart is a sister ship of mine. I'll see it done right away.
As for the secret passage, I'm sure they've used it by now.
As for the Royal Navy's fleet, why don't we... Actually, let's discuss this in person later.
The road here is a bit difficult to traverse, so I'll get back to you later–
BEEP—
Aaand she hung up. She really does march to the beat of her own drum...
In a way, she's like Prinz Eugen. Or... worse, even.
BEEP—
Another call? This time, it's... Cardinal Richelieu?
Suspicious timing. Is it related to that hidden passage the Commander mentioned?
This is Richelieu speaking.
Commander, representatives of the World Expo. I'm happy to see you all safe.
Our fleet has already arrived at Toulon and confirmed the safety of Jean Bart's fleet.
Once we've cleaned up enemies around the port, performed maintenance, and resupplied, we plan to come and join you.
(It seems like they made it to Toulon through their hidden passage without issue.)
We initially went to Saint Helena to secure the Crown of the Holy See and rescue Marseillaise.
Upon inspecting the documents we found at the secret stronghold there, we found a large amount of material concerning Siren technology.
One of the topics of interest was a network of "highways" that Compiler used to rapidly move across the ocean.
We then found a set of equipment called the "microlayer transference system," devices that would allow us to use Compiler's network in the form of sea fog.
While it is just normal fog, the activation of the device turns the microlayer particles comprising it into a gateway of sorts that allows us to move en-masse to distant locations.
Apparently, this network spans all over the world, which Compiler used to connect countless underwater areas and Mirror Seas.
Presumably, this is how Sirens used Mirror Seas as the staging grounds for their attacks, able to move their forces over long distances.
It also explains how they've always seemed to appear out of thin air, and were able to attack areas that should've been inaccessible to them.
In any case, this technology definitely was not meant for our use, but can still function without Compiler's authorization.
For whatever reason, following Compiler's defeat, we have been able to gain access into the network.
After studying the Tribunal's analytical data on Saint Helena, we were able to tap into the network, though only to a limited extent due to the limitations of our equipment.
We understand the inherent risks in using Siren technology and planned to report our findings first, but the situation changed when we learned that fierce fighting had broken out in Toulon.
The fact that we were able to lead such a large fleet through safely is surely due to the Holy Iris's blessings.
(It sounds to me like Clemenceau ordered the Tribunal to tell Richelieu the location of the Crown of the Holy See.)
(The crown might have ended up in Marco Polo's hands, but this alone is probably proof enough that Clemenceau isn't our enemy.)
(Tell her about Clemenceau.)
"I think you might not have a full grasp of the situation either, but we want to know as much as possible before we come face-to-face with Clemenceau."
Really? Clemenceau did that...?
Very well. I will tell you what I know.
Before setting sail, Clemenceau told me a part of her plan.
Her plan was to both stop Marco Polo and use this dangerous situation to bring Iris Libre and the Vichya Dominion back together.
...And by making herself the villain, she would protect Jean Bart.
Comrades, if I may offer my opinion–
Clemenceau's actions may still be suspicious, but I believe that we should have faith in our Commander and in Richelieu.
With the situation in the Mediterranean Sea—no, the entire world—as it is, the Tribunal's power and knowledge will be essential.
While they might have been split up as a product of the Reenactments, let us not forget that they once sailed under the single banner of the Iris Orthodoxy.
Let's leave our doubt and our worries of a betrayal that may have never happened aside. We must focus on the danger before us.
"Thanks for your input, Avrora."
A fair question. Between Jean Bart's repairs and resupplying...
...Oh. It seems enemy reinforcements have arrived.
Apologies, Commander, but it seems we'll need some more time.
Considering Clemenceau's behavior so far, I understand your reluctance to trust her.
But still... I ask that you look after her all the same, Commander.

The False God's Cocoon.say: A mixed fleet consisting of Sakuran and Iris forces just so happened to be nearby when Marco Polo's throne was shot out of the sky.
Said fleet was originally sent there to clear the surrounding area of enemies. They began their search for the wreckage once they'd confirmed the Horseman had ceased operation.
It didn't matter whether Marco Polo had lost her mind or just been manipulated – she had to be rescued.
I think the throne is somewhere around here! Keep your eyes peeled, everyone!
(While the whole sky darkened after that explosion, the area ahead feels even darker than its surroundings.)
(It's as though something is blocking the light... Or absorbing it, like a black hole?)
This is a good time to turn on my searchlights!
Now it'll be easy to–
Shimakaze's searchlights lit up the sea around her – and also revealed a misty hole in space.
Whoa, it's pitch black! Is it... swallowing up the light?
That's just like a black hole, isn't it?
Though the light could not pierce the darkness, it allowed everyone to clearly see its outline.
It's like an egg, jutting out of the water... Does that mean there's more of it underwater?
I think I remember reading about a sacrament that roughly matched this thing's description.
We've already established that the unknown substance is what's creating all the enemies.
So, it's possible that this other unknown substance produces a different breed of enemy.
The clouds swallow our aircraft and spit out twisted versions of them, while the cocoons produce enemies reminiscent of mass-produced ships.
The aircraft were liquid, and the mass-produced ships were solid. Therefore, perhaps this hole creates gaseous enemies?
It would explain why your searchlights are ineffective if the gas is really, really, REALLY dense.
Assuming this thing does spawn more enemies, we should destroy it now before it becomes fully operational.
What do you think, Shinano? Should we attack?
This one is inclined to do so... Lady Jeanne, what do you think?
On the one hand, we should study what we're dealing with. On the other, I agree with Noshiro.
I think that we should back off and bombard it with our aircraft.
That way, we can retreat quickly if something happens, and if Marco Polo for some reason is inside it, she'll suffer less damage than from direct cannon fire.
So be it... Hakuryuu, prepare your bombers...
On it. Been waiting too long as it is to use them.
KABOOOM!
Hakuryuu's aircraft dropped their bombs on the foggy cocoon, engulfing it in explosions.
The very next moment, it retaliated with a shockwave.
It took everyone. Hakuryuu, Noshiro, Shinano, Jeanne... the black mist engulfed every shipgirl who stood there.
Inside a strikingly decorated office room, Marco Polo sat at her desk with a defeated expression.
Another failure...
And that one, and that, and this one too... They've all failed...
How is it possible that I, the one born to spread Sardegna's glory, fail time after time after time again?
I don't get it... Why won't anyone understand me?
I want to restore the Empire's glory, I want to spread it all across the world, but nobody empathizes with my cause no matter the approach I take!
Not the carefree Veneto sisters, not those who are perfectly happy languishing away in mediocrity...
I'm so tired... Is there nothing I can do?
She fell limp onto her desk, and she took hold of an ornament thoughtlessly left lying around.
This cross must be ancient. Has it always been lying on my desk?
She fixated her gaze on the small cross, as if at this moment, there was no object in all the world more worthy of her attention.
Is this... a sign from God?
He has the power to destroy the Sirens...
Yes... That's it! The answer's been there all along!
The enemy of the Sirens... The one whose power is greater than theirs!
Ahahahahahaha!

Clemenceau's Aims.say: When Shinano's fleet attacked the black hole made by the gaseous substance, they were suddenly pulled deeper into the darkness.
*cough* *cough*... Shinano! Are you okay?!
Yes... Worry about the others instead—
Shinano, we're in a real mess!
Some weird enemies are crawlin' out of that dark goop!
...Fragments of nightmares, echoes of ruination...
They only show themselves in this one's dreams of death... Why are they here?
This is an ill omen... Jeanne, take everyone and evacuate...
It will be done. Suffren, Maillé Brézé! Turn about-face and secure an escape route—
No, Jeanne... We mustn't send anyone ahead on their own.
Going the way we came, too, is unnecessary... If this one's hypothesis is correct, this boundary is like a dream...
If we choose a specific direction and muster all of our strength, we may escape from this nightmarish domain...
Nightmares may be formless, but... If we have been drawn into one from the real world, then there must exist a border between them...
The enemy knows this, and they will try to surround us... Still, if we can break through, we may return to our home world...
If you're so familiar with this, Shinano, I'd like you to choose the direction for our charge.
"Nightmares are formless, but they have borders." Oversimplified, but surprisingly close to the truth.
However, I have a better plan.
I know the safest route. Come with me.
Who are you...?
I'm... a friend of the Commander.
I'll explain later. Just trust me for now.
If we waste any more time, we'll miss our chance to escape.
...And that's how I saved your friends. Couldn't have come at a better time too.
The abominations they saw are able to contaminate a shipgirl's hull upon contact.
Their courage is commendable, but if they aren't careful, it'll be their downfall.
But don't worry. The contact was limited, so there shouldn't be any issues after we finish decontamination.
Having that said, you should keep your distance for the time being if you value your safety.
Now where were we? Yes, it's great to see you again, Commander.
I hear you've been in the Superimposition—er... Reality Lens? Of late.
I can't believe things turned out this way.
...Though personally, I'm a little glad.
Upon learning that we'd lost contact with Shinano's fleet, I had put together a team to go rescue them. Just then, Memphis META had contacted us.
(Come to think of it, the other Helena helped us alongside this Memphis at the Union's Sea of Stars. I don't think I've had the chance to thank her...)
Are you the one who helped us at the Sea of Stars?
The Commander told me all about what happened in the Reality Lens.
Thank you, umm... Memphis?
...It's my pleasure.
Your Commander is my Commander, too, so it's only natural that I try to help.
*sigh*... At this rate, calling us both Memphis is going to get really confusing.
What should I call you?
Whatever you want to.
Memphis is fine, META is fine, "that girl who likes the Commander a little TOO much" is also fine.
Heh. Maybe that last one is true for both of us, though?
Th-that's not...!
My, my. I'll admit I was worried when you asked me to rescue your friend...
But you all are so carefree that I have to assume the problem is solved~ That's lovely.
Clemenceau inserted herself in the midst of the increasingly awkward conversation without anyone's prior notice.
Oh, this is embarrassing...
It's my fault. Now isn't the time for jokes.
Allow me to continue. Commander, the situation is extremely bad.
A hundred times worse than what you experienced in the Reality Lens.
Our Helena is doing her best to slow down the superimposition of worlds, but at this rate, it won't be enough.
You can't imagine how hard I had to work just to get to this branch.
Wait... You know about superimposition?
Well, well. Things are getting wilder than I'd imagined. I'm surprised you know about these things.
That should be my line. Someone from this branch knows about the superimposition of worlds?
...Are you involved with the person behind this?
I am, though only partially.
Marco Polo brought this plan to me, and I pretended to play along.
However, I am in the dark when it comes to what her "God" is.
What I do know is the principle by which she intends to have "God" descend and establish her false Paradise.
Naturally, I know how to stop it as well.
With that, Clemenceau produces a map.
The location where the abnormalities began, the Mediterranean Sea, is circled with a black pen.
A number of cities, ports, and islands are also circled, all of them interconnected with lines.
The large circle is her Paradise, where God will descend. That's the area currently filled with Divine Vestige.
I believe the small circles here are conceptual anchors meant to create and maintain the domain.
Conceptual anchors...?
Yes, they would be one of Vichya's Sacraments. They strengthen the manifestation of concepts, though I'm not sure that means anything to you.
For the moment, just consider them special devices that maintain the domain.
If we destroyed them... No, there are too many, and they're all too far apart.
They span every corner of the Mediterranean, and some beyond that. There's no way we could reach all those places in such a short time!
Indeed. Consider that proof of how long Marco Polo has been working on her scheme.
However, the grander the plan, the more openings there are for me to play a little prank~
Now, take a look at the lines connecting conceptual anchors.
Clemenceau traces the lines on the map with a finger.
Commander, Richelieu has told you about how she moved her fleet to Toulon, yes?
With Compiler's defeat, we've gained access to her microlayer transference network.
So, do you now understand what these lines on the map indicate?
While working with Marco Polo, I fudged the locations of the conceptual anchors just enough to bring them closer to the network's nodes.
You'll find that they're rather accessible now, given the new tools we have.
As for the conceptual anchors themselves... There's no need to destroy them. We'll merely subsume them into a different conceptual framework.
This ritual is meant to reflect Marco Polo's concept of God, but we will counteract that with the concept of true divinity.
